overview: This deck leverages green mana acceleration and card advantage to outpace opponents in the mid-to-late game. By combining token generation with graveyard recursion, it builds an overwhelming board presence that is difficult to clear. The black and red splashes provide efficient removal and aggressive pressure to close games quickly.
win_conditions: Overwhelming the opponent with a wide board of tokens and recursed creatures
Drawing into precise answers while maintaining card advantage through Indomitable Creativity
Returning large threats from the graveyard with Archon of Cruelty to race or close out the game

keep_criteria: Hands containing two or more lands and an early play like Indomitable Creativity
Hands with Wrenn and Six and enough lands to cast it by turn three
Hands with early interaction or recursion to protect key threats

Keep hands with 2-3 lands (preferably including fetchable duals or a Wrenn and Six), plus at least one of Indomitable Creativity, Archon of Cruelty, or a hand disruption spell (Thoughtseize/Inquisition).
Keep hands featuring Wrenn and Six paired with a land.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with zero or one land
Hands lacking early plays or card draw
Hands heavy on high-cost finishers without ramp
Mulligan to hands with four or more lands, multiple copies of low-impact spells, or no viable turn 1-2 plays.

early_game: Prioritize land drops and cast Indomitable Creativity to establish board presence and draw cards
Use red and black spells for early disruption or burn to slow aggressive opponents
Turn 1: Play a land, hold for Thoughtseize/Inquisition, or cast Wrenn and Six if mana allows.
Turn 2: Activate Wrenn and Six for colored mana or a 2/2 creature, or deploy Archon of Cruelty if ahead.
Sequence lands to ensure red/green mana for Indomitable Creativity by turn 3-4.
Prioritize fetching lands to enable Wrenn and Six's abilities and maintain flexibility.
mid_game: Deploy Wrenn and Six to untap lands and accelerate into larger threats
Cast Archon of Cruelty to begin recycling key creatures and apply flying pressure
Use Wrenn and Six to fix mana and draw cards, but avoid overcommitting to a single ability.
Indomitable Creativity requires a valuable target, so avoid filling it with low-impact cards unless you are committed to the token win condition.
Archon of Cruelty should be used to control the board while protecting your combo pieces.
Commit threats when you have interaction or a clear path to victory.
Hold Indomitable Creativity until you have a game-winning spell in the graveyard and sufficient mana to cast it safely.
late_game: Flood the board with tokens and recursed threats to create unblockable attackers
Use card advantage to outlast control decks and race midrange opponents
Indomitable Creativity casting a high-impact spell (such as a large creature, board wipe, or combo piece) from the graveyard, combined with the Saproling tokens, to overwhelm the opponent.
Archon of Cruelty can also close games if the board is open and the opponent lacks removal.
Go all-in when you have a clear path to victory: Indomitable Creativity in hand, a valuable spell in the graveyard, enough mana to cast it, and the opponent lacks interaction.
Commit when you can safely resolve your combo or when the board state favors your token army.

key_cards.name: Indomitable Creativity
key_cards.notes: Cast on opponent's turn to surprise them with a 4/4 body and card draw, or use it to refill your hand after playing big spells.

key_cards.name: Wrenn and Six
key_cards.notes: Use its untap ability to cast multiple spells in a turn, and leverage the saproling creation for additional blockers or recursion fuel.

key_cards.name: Archon of Cruelty
key_cards.notes: Let it die or sacrifice it intentionally to return high-impact creatures from your graveyard, creating a snowball effect with token generation.

sequencing_tips: Hold up black and red mana for interaction before committing to big green spells
Use Wrenn and Six to untap lands specifically for casting Archon of Cruelty or Indomitable Creativity
Time Archon of Cruelty's death to coincide with having key threats in your graveyard for maximum impact
Play Indomitable Creativity in response to removal to maintain board presence and card advantage

Common mistakes include overcommitting to Wrenn and Six's abilities too early, filling the graveyard with useless cards before casting Indomitable Creativity, and mismanaging colored mana for the combo.
Always track the opponent's graveyard and hand to time your spells correctly.
Archon of Cruelty is both a threat and a utility card
use it to control the board while protecting your combo pieces.
Remember that Indomitable Creativity's token generation scales with graveyard size, so curate your graveyard deliberately rather than treating it as a free win button.

matchups.advice: Ramp aggressively and use recursion to outvalue their hand traps. Keep early interaction to protect Wrenn and Six.
Use tokens as early blockers and draw into efficient removal. Race with Archon of Cruelty once you stabilize.
Establish a board presence quickly and use card advantage to dictate the pace. Recycle threats to pressure their resources.

Use Wrenn and Six to generate mana and draw around counterspells. Cast Indomitable Creativity when the opponent is tapped out or in response to their counterspells if you have priority. Sideboard cards that protect your spells or remove countersources can also help.

Apply early pressure with Archon of Cruelty and discard to disrupt their mana base. Use spot removal to kill their early threats. Do not let them stabilize; keep the board clear and race them to their late-game finishers.

Win the game of attrition by out-drawing and out-resolving. Use discard to disrupt their combo setup. Prioritize graveyard hate and artifact removal. Win by resolving Indomitable Creativity first or by out-valuing with Archon of Cruelty and Wrenn and Six.

common_threats: Early burn spells that target your face or ramp pieces
Board wipes that clear your token army
Hand disruption that removes key combo pieces before they resolve
Efficient removal spells that answer your Archon of Cruelty
Monitor combo decks (Ruby Storm, Jeskai Blink) for graveyard setup and hand resources.
Against ramp (Amulet Titan), track early mana rocks and large threats to decide when to stabilize.
Against control, track removal and counterspells to time your combo resolution.
Always count the opponent's available interaction to avoid dead-angling your Creativity.
